ops.title: Preferences and plans use shape-only validation, not explicit versioning
ops.body: `~/.getaway/preferences.json` and `~/.getaway/plans/<slug>.json` carry no `version` field — this is a deliberate user ruling (2026-07-11, commit 1920b346fdf3), not an omission. Validation is shape-only: `prefs-set`/`plan-set` check the merged document against the shipped template (key subset + has-or-type per field + the `status` enum) and refuse to write on mismatch. A file that doesn't fit the current shape is re-initialized (`prefs-init` / `plan-new`) or hand-edited, never migrated: no version checks, no migration snippets, no back-compat readers, no CHANGELOG upgrade pointers. Do not re-add a version field "for safety" when changing a template — extend the shape validation instead, and let unfit files fail loud at the existing exit-3 paths.
ops.when: Before re-introducing version fields to preferences or plans, or when redesigning schema validation
